title: get_block_range: no guard against start > end
description: LightwalletdClient::get_block_range (client.rs lines 175-197) accepts start and end as u64 values with no validation that start <= end. Passing start > end sends an inverted BlockRange to the lightwalletd server. The server behavior for inverted ranges is implementation-defined (may return an empty stream, an error, or blocks in reverse order). The function should either validate and return Err if start > end, or document the server behavior for that case. There is no test covering this path.
